coreIssue: This junior Brand Designer performance conversation is about making the quality bar concrete enough for Carlos to apply in daily work.
openingQuestion: What does strong work at your level look like to you right now, and where does that still feel hardest to judge in your own projects?
listenFor: whether he names a specific recent project, revision, or artifact instead of speaking only in generalities
whether he can compare good versus not-yet-there work using observable details like clarity, craft, or stakeholder response
if they describe a concrete blocker such as unclear feedback, timing, or not knowing what to prioritize in revisions
avoid: do not turn the conversation into a verdict on taste without showing concrete examples tied to junior-level expectations
do not overload him with multiple development themes when one immediate practice would make the feedback usable
goodOutcome: You and Carlos have agreed what good junior Brand Designer work looks like in one or two observable dimensions and one near-term practice he will apply in upcoming design revisions.
suggestedAction: Before the 1:1, bring two recent Carlos artifacts—one closer to the bar and one not yet there—so you can compare them against the same concrete criteria.
